user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: OCBC Pledges $540B+ AUM by 2029 — HSBC WPB Change Velocity Must Match
talking_points.why_it_matters: OCBC's public 100% AUM doubling target over 3.5 years directly challenges HSBC WPB's APAC competitive position, putting Adam's change delivery timeline under renewed external pressure.
bullets: Sunny Quek, Head of Global Consumer Financial Services at OCBC, publicly committed to doubling wealth management AUM from $270B to over $540B by 2029, backed simultaneously by the HSBC Indonesia acquisition (~1,300 staff), AI-powered RM training for 900 advisors, and OCBC GENesis next-gen retention.

OCBC's strategy runs three transformation workstreams in parallel — acquisitive growth, AI enablement, and next-gen relationship building — mirroring the ambition of HSBC WPB's own APAC agenda and setting a public pace benchmark.

HSBC WPB's Q1 $38bn NNIA validates the wealth strategy; Adam's change programme is now the critical path to operational delivery of the counter-offensive against a newly galvanised and market-rewarded OCBC.

source_links.title: OCBC Outpacing DBS and UOB YTD

talking_points.headline: Broadridge Agentic AI in Production: 30% Day-1 Cost Cut Is the New Change Benchmark
talking_points.why_it_matters: With Broadridge's agentic AI delivering measurable 30% Day 1 cost reductions across 40+ institutional clients, HSBC's APAC change teams now have a concrete, externally validated benchmark for AI-enabled operational delivery.
bullets: Broadridge's platform is live covering automated trade fails management, account opening, real-time valuation exceptions, and customer inquiry automation — all at institutional scale, with no constant human instruction and processing $15 trillion in daily trading activity.

The deployment spans 40+ BPO clients processing millions of transactions monthly, proving that agentic AI in banking operations is achievable now — raising the bar for HSBC's own AI change delivery timeline in APAC.

New clients choose between full managed-services (Broadridge runs operations end-to-end) or standalone agentic deployment — both models directly relevant to Adam's insource vs. outsource AI delivery assessment for WPB APAC.

source_links.title: Broadridge Agentic AI at Institutional Scale
Broadridge AI Live Across Capital Markets

talking_points.headline: AIS 2026 HK: HSBC Commits to Stress-Test-Level Alts Due Diligence — Platform Must Deliver
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's private banking team publicly described stricter alternative investment selection at AIS 2026 HK, creating a platform capability expectation that Adam's change programme must close to avoid a credibility gap.
bullets: At AIS 2026 Hong Kong (600+ senior wealth managers), HSBC's private bank joined JPM, UBS GWM, and Julius Baer in committing to mapping client portfolio stress across scenarios, including unintended correlations — a capability that demands upgraded analytics and RM support tools.

Very few alts managers now meet the raised APAC private bank bar — scale, brand, and past returns are insufficient; banks are scrutinising capital-raising pace, manager discipline, and style consistency — a platform deliverable, not just an advisory judgement.

The structural shift from 'alts as aspirational' to 'alts as tactical core allocation' across 600+ senior attendees signals that HSBC must operationalise stress-testing, scenario analysis, and manager correlation monitoring as first-class RM platform features.

source_links.title: AIS 2026 HK: Private Banks Raise Bar for Alts
AIS 2026 HK: Full House as Alts Go Tactical

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Targets 100% Wealth Growth to $540B+ AUM by 2029
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC Head of Global Consumer Financial Services Sunny Quek announced a target to double wealth management AUM from $270B to over $540B by 2029, leveraging the HSBC Indonesia acquisition, AI-powered RM training for 900 advisors, and the OCBC GENesis next-gen retention programme — a simultaneous multi-workstream build-out directly in HSBC WPB's APAC territory.

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Outpacing DBS and UOB YTD — Wealth Strategy Premium
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC has returned 16.9% YTD versus 7.6% for UOB and 7.5% for DBS, reflecting investor confidence in OCBC's 'Next Frontier' wealth strategy — the HSBC Indonesia acquisition, 100% AUM growth target, and fee income growth of 23% YoY in Q1 2026 have driven a significant market premium over peers.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: AIS 2026 HK: Private Banks Sharpen Alts Criteria as Performance Disperses
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: At AIS 2026 Hong Kong, HSBC, JP Morgan, UBS GWM, and Julius Baer disclosed significantly tightened criteria for selecting alternative investment managers — scale and past returns are no longer sufficient, with banks now stress-testing client portfolios for unintended correlations and demanding manager discipline, capital-raising transparency, and style consistency over time.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: JP Morgan Private Bank 2026 Mid-Year Outlook: India and China Tech Lead Asia
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: JP Morgan Private Bank's 2026 Mid-Year Outlook ('Promise and Pressure') positions India and selective China tech — AI platforms, EVs, and consumer — as Asia's standout opportunities, with MSCI China +16% YTD returning to pre-'Liberation Day' levels and Indian equity earnings estimates now 'beatable' after eight months of decline.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: Partners Group Taps Bank of East Asia for First HK Private Markets Deal
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: Partners Group ($185B AUM) named Bank of East Asia ($113B banking assets) as its first Hong Kong-based distribution partner, giving BEA's wealth and HNW clients access to private markets investments — Partners Group's first distribution partner based in HK and a signal of deepening alternatives demand from Hong Kong's banking sector.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Multi-Asset: Equity Momentum Signals Are 'Max Bullish'
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Chief Multi-Asset Strategist Max Kettner told Bloomberg that momentum signals for global equities are at their most bullish level, driven by market breadth expansion and positive cross-asset signals — providing tailwinds for HSBC WPB's equity-linked wealth products and supporting client risk appetite for H2 2026.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Issues EUR 3.5bn Senior Notes Across 2029–2036 Maturities
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Holdings raised EUR 3.5bn in senior unsecured notes on 12 May 2026 across three tranches — EUR1.25bn at 4.086% due 2036, EUR1.25bn at 3.553% due 2031, and EUR1bn floating-rate notes due 2029 — admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange, signalling active balance sheet management ahead of H2 planning.

AI & Technology.title: Broadridge Agentic AI Live Across 40+ Institutions: 30% Ops Cost Reduction
AI & Technology.summary: Broadridge's agentic AI platform went live in production across 40+ institutional BPO clients covering post-trade, account management, wealth operations, and customer inquiry automation — delivering up to 30% Day 1 operational cost reduction and processing millions of transactions monthly on a foundation of $15 trillion in daily trading activity.
